---
number-sections: false
---


In this workshop you will work through a workbook that guides you through how to work with these datasets and generate meaningful transport planning-related results. Exercises will stretch more advanced attendees.


## Getting Started

The quickest way to get started is on GitHub Codespaces, which you can launch by clicking the link below:

[![Open in GitHub Codespaces](https://github.com/codespaces/badge.svg)](https://codespaces.new/tdscience/tartu26?quickstart=1)

See the [prerequisites](prerequisites.qmd) for more details on how to set up your environment.

## Workshop Workbook

The main practical content is in the [workbook](workbook.qmd).

## Further reading

If you enjoyed this course, you may want to check out some of the links below:

- The [Data Science for Transport Planning website](https://tdscience.github.io/dstp/) with more sessions taught over 2 days.
- The [Transport Data Science](https://itsleeds.github.io/tds) course materials from the University of Leeds, which cover a wider range of topics and datasets.
- ... Please let us know of more materials in the [issue tracker](https://github.com/tdscience/tartu26/issues)
